political.s: SoulCycle faced significant backlash in 2019 when majority owner Stephen Ross hosted a Trump fundraiser, prompting boycott threats. The company issued a statement distancing itself from the event and subsequently launched instructor-led charitable rides. However, no formal corporate PAC or lobbying activity has been identified.

charity.s: SoulCycle operates charity ride programs raising over $2.5M annually and runs SoulScholars, a 12-week free program for underserved youth in NYC, Chicago, DC and LA. The company also hosts Give Back Rides where proceeds benefit causes selected by instructors.
charity.focus: Youth education and wellness, racial justice, community development

environment.s: SoulCycle announced SOUL Green sustainability initiative with goal of carbon neutrality by 2025. Actions include eliminating single-use plastics, switching to sustainable materials, partnering with eco-friendly suppliers, and transitioning apparel to recycled materials. However, progress toward stated 2025 carbon neutral goal is not independently verified.

labor.s: Employee reviews are mixed but lean negative. Glassdoor rating of 3.4/5 with only 40% recommending the company. Employee surveys show 27% agree they are paid fairly and most report no raises. Reports indicate minimum wage compensation for studio crew ($12.53-$15.61/hr), though HQ roles pay higher. Company faced allegations of racist and homophobic instructor behavior in 2020 with inadequate response.

dei.s: SoulCycle initiated DEI programs following 2020 racial justice protests, expanding training and creating BIPOC/Queer employee resource groups. Leadership partnered with D&I expert Dr. Tony Byers. Company created Director of DEI role and amplified BIPOC instructor voices. However, previous allegations of instructor racism/homophobia and employee diversity concerns remain.

animals.s: Not applicable - fitness service company with no animal testing, animal product use, or related concerns identified.

guns.s: Not applicable - indoor cycling fitness studio with no direct involvement in firearm sales or manufacture.

privacy.s: Mozilla Foundation rated SoulCycle privacy practices poorly, citing vague language and extensive data collection. Company purchases data from brokers and shares personal information with advertising partners. Collects workout preferences, location, social media data. While GDPR-compliant for UK users, overall practices flagged as concerning regarding data broker relationships.

execPay.s: Significant pay disparity exists between executives and studio workers. VP-level positions estimated at $280K+ while studio crew earn $37-40K annually. Average HQ employee compensation ~$119K versus studio front desk at ~$40K. Specific CEO-to-worker ratio not publicly disclosed but estimated at 7:1 or higher.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
